Chestnut.notes: Produced 1999–present. Renamed from Indian Red in 1999.

Macaroni and Cheese.notes: Produced 1993–present. Also found as "Macaroni & Cheese" and "Macaroni-n-Cheese".

Maize.notes: Produced 1903–1990. Known as "Gold Ochre", 1903–1958. "Golden Ochre" on some labels.

Goldenrod.notes: Produced 1903–present. Known as "Medium Chrome Yellow" (1903–?) and "Medium Yellow" (1903–1958).

Lemon Yellow.notes: Produced 1903–1990. Also known as "Light Chrome Yellow" (on labels "Chrome Yellow, Light") or "Light Yellow", 1903–1958. On labels "Chrome Yellow, Light."

Light Chrome Green.notes: "Chrome Green, Light" on labels. Produced 1903–1935. Same color as "Light Green" (1903–1935).

Medium Chrome Green.notes: "Chrome Green, Medium" on labels. Produced 1903–1939. Same color as "Medium Green" (1903–1939).

Pine Green.notes: Produced 1903–1949, 1958–present. Known as "Dark Chrome Green" ("Chrome Green, Dark" on labels) or "Dark Green", 1903–1949.

variants.name: Sky Blue with Glitzy Gold Glitter
Blue Green with Glitzy Gold Glitter

Maximum Blue.notes: Produced 1926–1958. Part of the Munsell line, 1926–1944. Also known as "Blue-Green", 1930–1958.

Purple Mountains' Majesty.notes: Produced 1993–present. Also found as "Purple Mountain Majesty" and "Purple Mountain's Majesty."

variants.name: Royal Purple with Ruby Red Glitter
Red Violet with Glitzy Gold Glitter

Peach.notes: Produced 1903–present. Known as "Flesh Tint" (1903–1949), "Flesh" (1949–1956, 1958–1962), and "Pink Beige" (1956–1958).

Gold (I).notes: Metallic; swatch represents nominal hue only. Produced 1903–1944. Available only in bulk after 1915.

Gold (II).notes: Metallic; swatch represents nominal hue only. Produced 1953–present. Available only in bulk, 1953–1956.

Silver.notes: Metallic; swatch represents nominal hue only. Produced 1903–present. Available only in bulk, 1915–1944.

Copper.notes: Metallic; swatch represents nominal hue only. Produced 1903–1915, 1958–present.

Antique Brass.notes: Metallic; swatch represents nominal hue only. Produced 1998–present.

Gray.notes: As "Middle Grey", part of the Munsell line, 1926–1944. Spelled "Grey" on labels, but "Gray" on boxes. Also called "Neutral Grey", 1930–1956.
